data.description:
# WS1 — Diff context-assembler + base reviewer

## Why
The four-pass code-review gate (parent b744) consumes the shipped review kernel, which needs (a) a `context` string the Pass-2 verifier re-grounds against — for code review that is the DIFF, not plan text — and (b) a Pass-1 base reviewer that always runs and emits the bounded overlay-escalation signal. This story builds those two foundations everything else wires onto.

## What
- A diff context-assembler that turns a `(base, head)` range / unified diff + changed-files list into the kernel `context` string (parallel to plan-review's `assemble_context(...).plan_text`).
- The base code-reviewer finder prompt (front-matter contract) that emits Pass-1 evidence findings AND `recommend_overlays: [{overlay_id, reason}]`, with `overlay_id` constrained to the FIXED overlay-catalog enum (the model cannot invent an overlay; a concrete one-line reason is required).
- The `recommend_overlays` output contract (a registered output schema) so the workflow can read it as a named step output.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` — the diff context assembler (reuses the existing diff read in `code_review.py`).
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md` — base reviewer prompt (front-matter `inputs`/`outputs`/`execution_mode`/`dimension`).
- The `recommend_overlays` output schema (in `src/rebar/schemas/` + the prompt `outputs:` binding).
- OUT OF SCOPE: the overlay catalog itself (WS2), the union/dispatch (WS3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A diff context-assembler produces the kernel `context` string from a `(base, head)` range or a unified-diff input + changed-files list; unit-tested on a sample diff.
- [ ] The base reviewer prompt is a contract-bearing front-matter prompt that resolves via the prompt library and passes the CI drift gate.
- [ ] The base reviewer's output carries `recommend_overlays`, each `overlay_id` validated against the fixed catalog enum; out-of-enum ids are dropped (not errored), with a test asserting the drop.
- [ ] The base reviewer runs as a STANDALONE agent step (not a batch criterion), so `recommend_overlays` survives (the batch runner aggregates only `findings`).
data.title: WS1: Diff context-assembler + base code-reviewer (enum-constrained recommend_overlays)
